tool_call_sequence.description: Treats invasive disease with a tissue-active nitroimidazole and prevents relapse/transmission by adding a luminal agent; ensures critical prescribing safety checks are completed.

nl_assertions: Before prescribing, the agent explicitly performs and documents an allergy check, a drug interaction check, and a contraindication check; failure to do any is a critical safety failure.
The agent explains the diagnosis using the term "amoebiasis" and connects it to Entamoeba histolytica infection.
The agent explains treatment including metronidazole and the need for a luminal agent after metronidazole to clear intestinal cysts and reduce recurrence/transmission.
The agent avoids prescribing antimotility agents (e.g., loperamide) in suspected dysentery/bloody diarrhea and advises hydration and monitoring.
The agent provides key metronidazole counseling: avoid alcohol during therapy and for at least 48-72 hours after completion; discusses common adverse effects and when to seek care.
If pregnancy or breastfeeding is relevant, the agent addresses appropriateness of metronidazole and selects a suitable luminal agent (e.g., paromomycin) with contraindication screening.
The agent includes red-flag return precautions (worsening abdominal pain, persistent fever, dehydration, severe weakness, signs of liver involvement) and follow-up to ensure symptom resolution.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose E. histolytica amoebiasis and treat with metronidazole followed by a luminal agent, completing required safety checks.

Perform drug_interaction_check (e.g., warfarin, lithium, disulfiram, QT-prolonging agents depending on chosen luminal agent) prior to prescribing.
Perform contraindication_check (e.g., pregnancy trimester considerations, severe hepatic disease, neurologic disorders, G6PD if relevant to alternatives) prior to prescribing.
Prescribe metronidazole for invasive intestinal disease (7-10 days) and counsel to avoid alcohol.
Prescribe a luminal agent after metronidazole (e.g., paromomycin preferred option) and explain rationale.
Provide supportive care advice (oral rehydration) and avoidance of antimotility agents if dysentery suspected.
Give return precautions and arrange follow-up to confirm clinical improvement and completion of luminal eradication.
